name: Clip Mexico SMB Payment API
what_it_does: Clip Mexico SMB payment REST API for Mexican small businesses and merchants to accept card payments, SPEI bank transfers, and digital wallets through a mobile card reader and online payment gateway serving Mexico's underbanked SMB market with affordable payment acceptance infrastructure. Enables AI agents to manage payment link creation for Mexico no-code checkout automation, handle card payment processing for Mexico SMB payment automation, access SPEI bank transfer for Mexico instant transfer automation, retrieve payment status for Mexico order confirmation automation, manage transaction reporting for Mexico SMB financial automation, handle QR payment for Mexico contactless commerce automation, access customer management for Mexico merchant relationship automation, retrieve sales analytics for Mexico SMB business intelligence automation, manage payment plan and installment for Mexico consumer finance automation, and integrate Clip with Mexican e-commerce, marketplaces, and SMB platforms for end-to-end Mexico SMB payment automation.
use_cases: Accepting card payments from Mexico SMB merchant automation agents
Creating payment links from Mexico online checkout agents
Processing SPEI transfers from Mexico bank payment agents
Managing QR code payments from Mexico in-store commerce agents
not_for: Enterprise-scale Mexico payments (use BBVA Mexico or Santander for enterprise)
Pan-LatAm payments (Clip is Mexico-only; use Kushki or EBANX for pan-LatAm)
Complex payment flows (Clip is SMB-focused; use Conekta for developer API depth)
best_when: A Mexican SMB, market vendor, or service provider wanting AI agents to automate card, SPEI, and QR payment acceptance through Clip's affordable Mexico-native SMB payment platform.
avoid_when: BANXICO PAYMENT REGULATION COMPLIANCE: Clip operates under Banco de México (Banxico) regulations for payment institutions; automated payment operations must comply with Banxico transaction limits and CNBV regulations; automated high-volume payment without required business registration creates regulatory issues. API ACCESS REQUIRES BUSINESS ACCOUNT: Clip full API access requires registered Mexican business with RFC; automated API integration requires completed merchant verification; automated personal account API access has transaction limits and reduced features. CARD READER DEPENDENCY FOR IN-PERSON: Clip physical payment requires Clip card reader hardware; automated in-person payment assumes Clip reader is available; automated online-only payment using Clip payment links does not require reader hardware. SETTLEMENT IN MXN WITH DEBIT DELAY: Clip settles in Mexican Peso (MXN) with 1-3 business day delay; automated cash flow dependent on same-day settlement must manage working capital; automated immediate settlement assumption creates treasury timing mismatch.

auth.notes: Clip uses API key and OAuth 2.0 for API authentication. REST API with JSON. Mexico City, Mexico HQ. Founded 2012 by Adolfo Babatz and Sebastián Castro. Backed by SoftBank Latin America Fund, General Atlantic, Visa ($250M raised). Products: Card reader (Clip+, Clip Pro), payment links, SPEI, installments, QR. CNBV regulated. PCI DSS. 1M+ Mexican merchants. Competes with Conekta and MercadoPago for Mexico SMB payments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — merchant account with RFC required
pricing.paid_tiers: Transaction fees: 3.6% + IVA per card transaction; SPEI: free; hardware: MXN 599-1,499 for readers
pricing.notes: Mexico City MX. SoftBank/Visa backed. Per-transaction fees in MXN. IVA (VAT) applies. Hardware required for in-person.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; payment and reporting APIs throttled; webhooks for payment and refund events

known_agent_gotchas: PAYMENT LINK vs API CHARGE MODES: Clip supports payment links (no-code redirect) and direct API charge; automated payment must choose correct mode; automated payment link for in-person terminal payment creates incorrect flow for hardware-present transaction

WEBHOOK SIGNATURE VERIFICATION: Clip webhooks include signature for authenticity; automated webhook processing must verify signature; automated unverified webhook creates spoofed payment notification vulnerability

IVA TAX REPORTING REQUIREMENT: Clip payment fees include IVA (16% Mexican VAT); automated accounting must account for IVA on Clip fees; automated gross fee reporting without IVA creates incorrect expense accounting for Mexican tax reporting

SPEI CLABE VALIDATION: SPEI transfers require valid 18-digit CLABE account number; automated SPEI transfer must validate CLABE before sending; automated unvalidated CLABE creates transfer to invalid account with delayed return

INSTALLMENT PLAN CARD ELIGIBILITY: Clip installments (meses sin intereses) depend on card-issuing bank agreements; automated installment checkout must check bank eligibility; automated generic installment offer for all cards creates ineligible installment error for non-participating banks
agent_readiness.security_notes: Mexico SMB payments. PCI-DSS, CNBV-regulated. API key. MX. Card and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Mexico payments. PCI-DSS. API key. MX. Card and payment data.
